Politics.headline: Armed man shot and killed outside Mar-a-Lago
Politics.detail: Law enforcement shot and killed an armed man outside of President Trump's Mar-a-Lago resort Sunday; Trump was not present at the time of the incident.

Politics.headline: Trump says he's raising new global tariff rate to 15% after Supreme Court loss
Politics.detail: After the Supreme Court struck down an earlier set of sweeping global tariffs, President Trump announced he would be raising the global tariff he wants to impose to 15%, up from the 10% he announced a day earlier.

Politics.headline: Judge permanently blocks release of Jack Smith's report on Trump documents case
Politics.detail: A federal judge has permanently blocked the Justice Department from releasing former special counsel Jack Smith's report on the classified documents investigation.

Politics.headline: Clashes in Albania as protesters demand PM Rama resign
Politics.detail: Violence has erupted near parliament in Albania as demonstrators demand accountability in a corruption investigation and the resignation of Prime Minister Rama.

Politics.headline: Gov. Wes Moore's redistricting plan is poised to die.
Politics.detail: Maryland Gov. Wes Moore's redistricting plan is facing likely failure.

Finance & Economy.headline: Trump Vows to Hike Global Tariffs to 15%
Finance & Economy.detail: After the Supreme Court blocked President Trump's emergency tariffs, he announced plans to raise global tariffs to a blanket 15%, which has pressured global markets. Bloomberg Economics calculated that this would mean an average effective tariff rate of around 12%.

Finance & Economy.headline: Asian Stocks Rally After Trump's Supreme Court Tariffs Blow
Finance & Economy.detail: Asian stocks mostly rose and the dollar fell after the U.S. Supreme Court struck down a large part of President Trump's tariffs policy; the rally was led by tech firms, as traders sought cheaper investments amid concerns about Wall Street valuations. Morgan Stanley economists say the weighted average tariff rate will fall to 17% from 20%, with average levies on goods from China declining to 24% from 32%.

Finance & Economy.headline: German Business Sentiment Improves More Than Expected
Finance & Economy.detail: German business sentiment improved more than expected in February, according to a survey released on Monday by the Ifo Institute.

Finance & Economy.headline: Private Equity's Dry Spell Now Worse Than 2008 Crisis, Bain Says
Finance & Economy.detail: Private equity returned fewer profits to investors for a fourth straight year as the industry sat on $3.8 trillion of unsold assets and struggled to raise money for new funds.

Finance & Economy.headline: Doug Gurr Named as Preferred Candidate for Permanent CMA Chair
Finance & Economy.detail: Doug Gurr was named the preferred candidate to continue as chair of the Competition and Markets Authority for a full five-year term on Monday, subject to a parliamentary pre-appointment hearing.

Technology.headline: Tesla slashes Cybertruck prices, adds $59,990 entry model with trimmed features
Technology.detail: Tesla has reduced the price of the Cybertruck by introducing a new entry-level model priced at $59,990, which includes some feature reductions to meet the new price point. The dual-motor variant offers a 325-mile range and a 4.1-second 0-60 mph time but with reduced towing and cargo capacity.

Technology.headline: Technology Innovation Institute Launches Cloud Access to In-House Quantum Processing Units
Technology.detail: The Technology Innovation Institute (TII) in Abu Dhabi has launched a cloud service providing access to its Quantum Processing Units (QPUs), enabling partners to run quantum workloads on TII's physical quantum hardware. This service allows for applied quantum research and hybrid quantum–classical experimentation on locally developed systems.

Technology.headline: Week 8 trending phones: Galaxy S26 Ultra leads as new entrants join chart
Technology.detail: Samsung's Galaxy S26 Ultra remains the top trending phone as its official announcement nears, while new entrants like vivo V70 and Infinix Note 60 Pro have joined the list. The Galaxy A56 also remains in the spotlight due to extended lifecycles driven by surging RAM prices.

Technology.headline: EU and Nigeria open negotiations on a bilateral Science & Technology Cooperation Agreement
Technology.detail: The European Commission and the Federal Republic of Nigeria have started negotiations for a Science & Technology Cooperation Agreement to boost collaboration on research and innovation. This agreement aims to establish a political framework for assessing past cooperation and determining future actions, aligning with the African Union (AU)-EU Innovation Agenda.

Technology.headline: Innoviz Technologies white paper on Physical AI World Models
Technology.detail: Innoviz Technologies released Part I of a white paper, "Innoviz and the Rise of Physical AI: Bringing World Models to Life", arguing that automotive-grade LiDAR provides the necessary 3D perception for Physical AI and synchronized World Models. The paper highlights LiDAR's advantages over cameras and radar and how InnovizSMART platforms can provide live spatial data to various systems.

Science.headline: Schrödinger's Color Theory Finally Completed After 100 Years
Science.detail: A century after Erwin Schrödinger sketched out a bold vision for how we perceive color, scientists have finally filled in the missing pieces.

Science.headline: Scientists Create Universal Nasal Spray Vaccine That Protects Against COVID, Flu, and Pneumonia
Science.detail: A new nasal-spray vaccine has shown promise against a variety of germs and a common allergen in an early animal test.

Science.headline: Blood test boosts Alzheimer's diagnosis accuracy to 94.5%, clinical study shows
Science.detail: A recent clinical study indicates that a blood test can significantly improve the accuracy of Alzheimer's diagnosis, reaching 94.5% accuracy.

Science.headline: Ant queen frozen in time: New ant species found in Dominican amber
Science.detail: A new ant species has been discovered in Dominican amber, perfectly preserved as if frozen in time.

Science.headline: Oldest Fossilized Butthole Found in 290-Million-Year-Old Reptile
Science.detail: Scientists have discovered the oldest fossilized butthole in a 290-million-year-old reptile.

Health.headline: Blood Test Predicts Alzheimer's Onset
Health.detail: Scientists have created a blood test that can estimate when Alzheimer's symptoms are likely to begin by measuring a protein called p-tau217. The model predicts symptom onset within roughly three years.

Health.headline: New OHSU study finds nearly a third of Medicaid-enrolled physicians don't see Medicaid patients
Health.detail: A new study from Oregon Health and Science University found that a third of physicians enrolled in Medicaid do not see Medicaid patients, and another third of physicians enrolled in Medicaid may be overburdened, with higher-than-average yearly patient volumes. These physicians are deemed “ghost” providers.

Health.headline: New Africa CDC–FHI 360 Partnership to Boost Public Health Systems Across Africa
Health.detail: Africa CDC and FHI 360 signed a Memorandum of Understanding to strengthen health security and build more resilient public health systems across the continent. Through this collaboration, they will support African Union Member States in strengthening systems to prevent, detect and respond decisively to public health threats.

Health.headline: Measles outbreak
Health.detail: South Carolina, Utah, and Arizona are experiencing widespread measles outbreaks, contributing to a nationwide tally of nearly 1,000 infections, which is outpacing 2025's case count. A regional health organization has also issued an alert as measles cases surge across the Americas.

Health.headline: AMA Helps Drive Positive Change in Health Care's Defining Moment
Health.detail: The American Medical Association (AMA) is advocating for patient access amid potential health insurance losses due to the One Big Beautiful Bill Act and related Affordable Care Act policies. The AMA also scored a victory in protecting beneficiaries of grants from the U.S. Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration after calling for the restoration of cuts.

Sports.headline: Eze, Gyokeres Destroy Tottenham To Boost Arsenal's Premier League Title Bid
Sports.detail: Arsenal crushed Tottenham 4-1, reigniting their title charge as Eberechi Eze haunted his former suitors again, putting Spurs in a tough spot near the relegation zone.

Sports.headline: Tyler Reddick wins at Atlanta to give Michael Jordan second straight NASCAR Cup victory
Sports.detail: Tyler Reddick secured a win at Atlanta, marking Michael Jordan's second consecutive NASCAR Cup victory, continuing a hot start to the 2026 season for the 23XI Racing team.

Sports.headline: USA beats Canada in OT for first men's hockey gold since 1980
Sports.detail: Team USA beat Canada in overtime to win their first men's hockey gold since 1980 at the Winter Olympics, in a thrilling match.

Sports.headline: Lakers unveil statue of Pat Riley, the coaching mastermind of their 1980s Showtime era
Sports.detail: The Los Angeles Lakers unveiled a statue of Pat Riley, honoring his legacy as the coaching mastermind behind the team's 'Showtime' era in the 1980s.

Sports.headline: Clingan scores 23 points to lead Trail Blazers past short-handed Suns 92-77
Sports.detail: Donovan Clingan scored 23 points and had 13 rebounds, leading the Portland Trail Blazers to a 92-77 victory over the Phoenix Suns, who were missing key players.

Entertainment.headline: BAFTA Awards 2026 highlights: A history-making win, Timothee Chalamet snubbed, a racial slur and Prince William's viral confession
Entertainment.detail: The 2026 BAFTAs saw some surprising moments, including a racial slur during the broadcast and Prince William making headlines. There was also a history-making win.

Entertainment.headline: Dipika Kakar health update: Actor breaks down as liver cancer reoccurs days before 8th anniversary, says anxious about son Ruhaan.
Entertainment.detail: Dipika Kakar shared a health update revealing her liver cancer has reoccurred, causing her anxiety before her 8th anniversary.

Entertainment.headline: Kate Middleton dazzles alongside Hollywood stars Kate Hudson and Emma Stone at the 2026 BAFTAs.
Entertainment.detail: Kate Middleton made an appearance at the 2026 BAFTAs, alongside Hollywood stars like Kate Hudson and Emma Stone.

Entertainment.headline: BBC and host apologize after racial slur shouted during BAFTA awards by guest with Tourette's
Entertainment.detail: The BBC and BAFTA have apologized after a racial slur was shouted during the British Academy Film Awards by a guest with Tourette's syndrome.

Entertainment.headline: Eric Dane's friends launch $250K GoFundMe for daughters after 'Grey's Anatomy' star loses ALS battle.
Entertainment.detail: Following Eric Dane's death from ALS, friends have launched a $250K GoFundMe campaign to support his daughters.

Crime & Justice.headline: Mexican cartel leader 'El Mencho' killed in military operation, violence erupts
Crime & Justice.detail: Nemesio Rubén Oseguera Cervantes, leader of the Jalisco New Generation Cartel (CJNG), was killed in a military operation, leading to widespread violence in Mexico, including roadblocks and vehicle fires in multiple states. The U.S. had offered a $15 million bounty for information leading to his capture.

Crime & Justice.headline: Mexico on alert after the fall of El Mencho
Crime & Justice.detail: Following the death of Nemesio “El Mencho” Oseguera Cervantes, leader of the Jalisco New Generation Cartel (CJNG), a dozen Mexican states suspended school classes, and the United States warned its citizens in nearly a third of the country not to leave their homes due to the wave of violence unleashed. The cartel responded with roadblocks and fire, causing widespread disruption.

Crime & Justice.headline: Cocaine bust nabs producer of girl group XG
Crime & Justice.detail: Tokyo police arrested Junho Sakai, the producer of the girl group XG, along with three others, for alleged possession of cocaine at a hotel in Aichi Prefecture. Police seized bags of white powder believed to be cocaine and plant matter believed to be dried cannabis.

Crime & Justice.headline: Prosecution: Duterte must be tried for killing of civilians, children
Crime & Justice.detail: At the International Criminal Court, prosecutors charged Rodrigo Duterte with three counts of murder as a crime against humanity, stating these cases represent only a fraction of the deaths during his time as Davao City mayor and Philippine president. The prosecution argues Duterte should be held accountable for his war on drugs, which resulted in the killings of thousands, including children.

Crime & Justice.headline: Every child caught with a knife to get tailored support
Crime & Justice.detail: In England and Wales, every child caught carrying a knife will receive a mandatory targeted plan aimed at preventing re-offending and protecting the public. The government will also collaborate with children to create a child-friendly version of the Victims' Code, ensuring swifter justice.

Education.headline: Any amendment to CRMI regulations requires statutory process: NMC on MBBS intern stipend disparity row
Education.detail: The Undergraduate Medical Education Board (UGMEB) of the National Medical Commission (NMC) has stated that any amendment to the existing rule regarding stipend disparity between MBBS interns in government and private medical colleges would require a statutory process. The CRMI Regulations, 2021 already provide for payment of stipend to interns, but the actual implementation and amount are decided by States and Union Territories.

Education.headline: Rajasthan starts mental health support in schools
Education.detail: The Rajasthan Education Department has started to strengthen mental health support in government schools, in view of rising stress and anxiety among students during examinations. The move aims to create a supportive, safe, and positive environment to ensure that students are academically competent, emotionally balanced, and confident.

Education.headline: One in 10 undergrads is in high school. Students have warnings and advice.
Education.detail: With dual enrollment's growing popularity, The Washington Post interviewed current and former students about how the experience has shaped them and what advice they have for would-be enrollees. Dual enrollment programs allow high school students to take college courses.

Education.headline: Sudbury Public Schools and Non-essential Town offices will be closed on Monday, February 23.
Education.detail: Due to a snowstorm, Sudbury Public Schools and non-essential town offices in Sudbury, MA, will be closed on Monday, February 23, 2026. Power outages are expected, and residents are advised to stay informed with local news and social media feeds.

Education.headline: U.S. Secretary of Education Linda McMahon Honors Louisiana Custodian Donella Wagner as 2026 RISE Award Honoree
Education.detail: U.S. Secretary of Education Linda McMahon named Donella Wagner, Head Custodian at Raintree Elementary School in Baldwin, Louisiana, as the 2026 National Recognizing Inspiring School Employees (RISE) Award honoree. The RISE Award honors classified school employees who provide exemplary service.

Lifestyle.headline: 6 planets will parade across the night sky at the end of February
Lifestyle.detail: Six planets will be visible in the night sky at the end of February, and most can be seen with the naked eye. The parade should be visible over the weekend and in the days after; look to the western sky about an hour after sunset.

Lifestyle.headline: Portugal home to TripAdvisor's world 5th best beach in 2026
Lifestyle.detail: Praia da Falésia in the Algarve has been ranked as the 5th best beach in the world by TripAdvisor. The rankings are based on the volume and quality of global traveller reviews over the past year, highlighting Portugal's appeal as a beach destination.

Lifestyle.headline: Lithuanian women outnumber men sixfold among centenarians, data shows
Lifestyle.detail: New data shows that women aged 100 and above in Lithuania outnumber men by roughly six to one. Experts cite biology, lifestyle, and social factors as key drivers of this longevity gap, with women generally maintaining healthier lifestyles.

Lifestyle.headline: Met Gala guests (and the rest of us) learn the next dress code: 'Fashion is Art'
Lifestyle.detail: The Metropolitan Museum of Art is opening new galleries for costume art, aiming to integrate fashion more fully into the museum experience. The new show, "Costume Art," will run from May 10, 2026, through Jan. 10, 2027, and hopes to inaugurate an era of collaboration within the museum that puts fashion forward.

Lifestyle.headline: She rebuilt a classic Chevy after the L.A. fires — but still gets asked if it's her dad's work
Lifestyle.detail: Carmen Vera restores classic cars, and recounts an experience where someone assumed the car was her dad's. Vera's shop restores cars, turning them "from junk to works of art".

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Even our favorite team has a human rights sportwashing problem
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Premier League soccer jerseys, like Arsenal's, promote airlines and countries with connections to human rights abuses, specifically the UAE's support for militias in Sudan.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Opinion: CT can choose to end child poverty
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Connecticut has the capacity and responsibility to use its resources to address child poverty through measures like universal no-cost school meals and stronger housing stability protections.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Promise in the New Year
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Taiwan has shown positive developments, including a record stock market close and the signing of the Taiwan-US Agreement on Reciprocal Trade, but must navigate challenges with strength and resilience.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial cartoon for Feb. 23, 2026
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Editorial cartoon for Feb. 23, 2026 from The Daily News.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The U.S. is sleepwalking into war with Iran. Trump won't explain why.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An opinion piece discussing the potential for escalating conflict between the U.S. and Iran.

World.headline: Mexican security forces kill drug cartel boss 'El Mencho'
World.detail: Nemesio Ruben Oseguera Cervantes, leader of the Jalisco New Generation Cartel (CJNG), was killed in a military operation, triggering violence across Mexico. The US is warning its citizens to shelter in place in some cities like Puerto Vallarta and Cancun.

World.headline: US orders non-emergency personnel to leave Beirut embassy amid tensions
World.detail: The US State Department has ordered the departure of non-emergency government employees and eligible family members from the U.S. Embassy in Beirut due to heightened tensions. Washington determined it was 'prudent to reduce our footprint to essential personnel' in Lebanon.

World.headline: Russia launches 50 missiles, almost 300 drones in overnight attack on Ukraine, says Kyiv
World.detail: Ukraine reported that Russia launched a large-scale aerial attack, involving 50 missiles and nearly 300 drones, targeting various regions across the country. The attacks occurred on the eve of the fourth anniversary of the war.

World.headline: Donald Trump Sneers At "Dumb" Tariff Verdict That Gave Him "More Powers"
World.detail: Donald Trump criticized the Supreme Court's ruling on his administration's tariffs, despite the ruling actually limiting his power. The US Customs and Border Protection agency will halt collections of tariffs imposed under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act on Tuesday.

World.headline: Chad shuts border with Sudan after cross-border incursion kills its troops
World.detail: Chad has closed its border with Sudan following a cross-border incursion that resulted in the deaths of Chadian troops. The incident further strains relations between the two countries.

Zimbabwe.headline: Trouble mounts for Chatunga Mugabe as missing gun sparks extra charges in Joburg
Zimbabwe.detail: Chatunga Mugabe, son of the late former President Robert Mugabe, faces additional charges in Johannesburg related to a missing gun, compounding his legal issues.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe no longer keen on raw mineral exports, says Mnangagwa
Zimbabwe.detail: President Mnangagwa has stated that Zimbabwe is shifting away from exporting raw minerals, signaling a move towards beneficiation and value addition within the country.

Zimbabwe.headline: Government crafts new system for appointments to parastatal boards
Zimbabwe.detail: The government is developing a new system for appointing individuals to parastatal boards, aiming to improve governance and efficiency in state-owned enterprises.

Zimbabwe.headline: The high life of Chatunga Mugabe: From the Blue Roof to Hyde Park mansions and courtrooms
Zimbabwe.detail: An article detailing the lifestyle of Chatunga Mugabe, son of the late former President Mugabe, highlighting his transitions from Zimbabwe to international locations and his recent legal troubles.

Zimbabwe.headline: War was about one man, one vote: Chiwenga
Zimbabwe.detail: VP Chiwenga says that the war was about one man one vote.
